meta.title: Drug-resistant epilepsy in post-traumatic epilepsy (Yu nomogram)
meta.authors: Yu T, Liu X, Sun L, et al.
meta.outcome: Probability of developing drug-resistant epilepsy (DRE) in people with post-traumatic epilepsy, from 4 factors
meta.cff_info:
What this shows. A nomogram (Yu et al., CNS Neurosci Ther 2022) estimating the probability of drug-resistant epilepsy (DRE) in people with post-traumatic epilepsy. Developed on 2023 and internally validated on 807 patients (overall DRE 21%; C-index ~0.66–0.69).

How to read it

  • Drivers: status epilepticus in the early course, focal or mixed seizure onset, epileptiform EEG, and younger age at the first post-traumatic seizure.

Single-centre, internally validated only, moderate discrimination — a demonstration tool.

predictors.name: Status epilepticus (early course)
predictors.assess: Status epilepticus during the first years of the post-traumatic epilepsy course. Present raises DRE risk (OR 3.63).

predictors.name: Seizure onset type
predictors.assess: Predominant seizure onset type (ILAE 2017). Focal (OR 1.74) and mixed (OR 2.34) carry higher DRE risk than generalized.

predictors.name: Interictal EEG
predictors.assess: Single interictal outpatient EEG. Epileptiform discharges raise DRE risk (OR 1.60).

predictors.name: Age at first post-traumatic seizure
predictors.assess: Age (years) at the first late post-traumatic seizure. Younger age carries higher DRE risk (OR 0.985 per year).

model.note: DRE = ILAE 2009 definition (failure of ≥2 tolerated, appropriately used ASMs). Post-traumatic epilepsy population. Single-centre (n=2830), internal validation only, C-index ~0.66–0.69.
model.viz_info: Each factor adds its published log-odds to a logistic model; the sum plus the intercept gives the probability of drug-resistant epilepsy.

provenance.source: Yu 2022 CNS Neurosci Ther Table 3 (β + intercept -1.695) — doi 10.1111/cns.13897, PMID 35822252
provenance.notes: Logistic intercept -1.695; β: age -0.015/yr, focal 0.551/mixed 0.850 (ref generalized), status-epilepticus 1.289, EEG abnormal-bg 0.287/epileptiform 0.469 (ref normal). Verified: age20/focal/SE/epileptiform 57.8%, age40/generalized/none/normal 9.2%. DRE rate 21%. C-index 0.66-0.69, single-centre. Built 2026-07-08.
